content: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is a neurodevelopmental disorder characterized by persistent deficits in social communication/interaction across contexts (social-emotional reciprocity, nonverbal communication, developing/maintaining relationships) and restricted/repetitive patterns of behavior, interests, or activities (stereotyped movements, insistence on sameness, highly restricted interests, sensory hyper/hyporeactivity). Symptoms present in early developmental period, cause clinically significant impairment. ASD is heterogeneous, ranging from requiring very substantial support to requiring support. Prevalence ~1-2%, male predominance (4:1). Pathophysiology involves genetic factors (heritability ~80%, hundreds of genes implicated), altered synaptic function, atypical neural connectivity.
